name: Sacred Heart Parish Hallettsville
description_short: Federal credit union in Hallettsville, TX with $58.3M in assets, serving 2,770 members since 1940 with faith-based membership eligibility.
description_long:
Sacred Heart Parish Hallettsville Federal Credit Union is a faith-based credit union established in 1940, making it over 86 years old. It is headquartered in Hallettsville, Texas and operates as a federally-chartered credit union with charter number 3946. The institution serves primarily members connected to Sacred Heart Parish through a multiple common bond membership model focused on faith-based affiliation. With approximately $58.3 million in total assets and 2,770 active members, the credit union operates a single branch location staffed by 8 full and part-time employees under the leadership of CEO Laurie A Janak and Chairperson Charles Haas.

The credit union offers standard banking and financial services typical of community-based credit unions. Members have access to checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, and a loan portfolio spanning real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and other credit products. The institution generated $1.36 million in loan income during the most recent reporting period and maintains a net worth of $6.16 million. The credit union also offers international remittances and low-cost wire transfers as ancillary services.

As a faith-based, member-owned cooperative, Sacred Heart Parish Hallettsville distinguishes itself through its parish-rooted mission and local community focus. Operating a single branch keeps overhead minimal and allows for personalized service. The credit union is not a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB) system and does not carry a low-income credit union designation. Its membership is restricted to those with a faith-based common bond affiliation, which creates a more cohesive, values-aligned membership base but limits accessibility.

This is a stable, modestly-sized regional credit union suitable for members within its faith-based community. Its longevity since 1940 demonstrates sustainability, and recent net income of $268,256 reflects operational profitability. However, with only one branch location and limited assets relative to larger credit unions, members should verify that the institution's product offerings and technology infrastructure meet their banking needs before joining. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: 86-year operating history demonstrates institutional stability and long-term viability since 1940
$58.3 million in assets with positive net income of $268,256 shows financial health
Offers low-cost wire transfers and international remittance services not always available at small institutions
Faith-based membership model creates aligned, values-driven community of 2,770 members
Single-branch local focus enables personalized service and community relationships
Loan portfolio of $31.1 million shows active lending to support member borrowing needs
cons: Only 1 branch location limits convenience and in-person access for members outside Hallettsville
Membership restricted to those with faith-based common bond affiliation; not open to general public
Not a Federal Home Loan Bank member, potentially limiting mortgage product flexibility and funding
Limited information on digital banking platform capabilities and mobile app availability
Small staff of 8 employees may create service bottlenecks during peak times
best_for: Faith-based community members in Hallettsville and surrounding areas seeking locally-rooted banking
Individuals needing international remittance services within a credit union environment
Members prioritizing relationship banking and personal service over branch convenience
Borrowers seeking real estate, personal, and other loans within a values-aligned institution
diagnosis: Sacred Heart Parish Hallettsville is best suited for faith-based community members in the Hallettsville area seeking stable, values-aligned banking with personalized service. The main caveat is membership eligibility—this credit union is not open to the general public and requires faith-based common bond affiliation, and its single branch location limits accessibility for those outside the immediate area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
